Role / Description: Produces Virginia-class Propulsor Forward Assemblies (pump-jet propulsor hardware) and provides related design-engineering, sustainment hardware, and spares. Public awards cover manufacture of fixed propulsor assemblies for multiple boats.

Newport News Shipbuilding is the Virginia-class co-builder with General Dynamics Electric Boat. It fabricates and outfits large submarine modules, joins pressure-hull sections, and performs assembly, test, and delivery work on boats assigned to Newport News.

Teledyne D.G. O'Brien supplies high-reliability electrical interconnect hardware for Virginia-class submarines. Its submarine products include pressure-hull penetrators, connectors, and outboard cable assemblies; public procurement data confirms class-specific parts/material support but does not disclose the exact subaward part numbers.

The exact Virginia-class deliverable could not be confirmed publicly. APCO's naval business designs, manufactures, integrates, and installs advanced assembly and welding fixtures used to fabricate naval structures and components.
NAICS-6 title: Not confirmed from reliable public sources

Role / Description: Produces the Circuit-D naval computer-controlled power-supply system, originally designed for the Virginia-class program and described as a critical platform electronic system. EMS also supplies parts and spares for advanced degaussing/signature-management systems.
NAICS-6 title: Capacitor, Resistor, Coil, Transformer, and Other Inductor Manufacturing

Role / Description: Supplies highly engineered naval-nuclear submarine fluid-system hardware, including proprietary valves, manifolds, pipe fittings, and flanges. Public filings identify these product lines as components for U.S. naval nuclear submarines.
NAICS-6 title: Fabricated Pipe and Pipe Fitting Manufacturing

Role / Description: Supplies marine accommodation/joiner systems and interior hardware for Virginia-class submarines. A hardware supplier states that Jamestown commissioned the class's stainless-steel 3811-series mortise door-lock system.

Designs and manufactures precision motors and generators for naval and submarine electrical machinery. The exact Virginia-class motor or generator model covered by the subaward was not publicly disclosed.

Provides shipbuilding and program-management advisory services, including design-for-affordability, production planning, coordination, and industry expertise. The exact Virginia-class task performed under the subaward was not publicly described.
NAICS-6 title: Administrative Management and General Management Consulting Services

Role / Description: Through Granite State Manufacturing, produces precision-machined, welded, and electromechanical submarine components. Public reporting on Block V work says its products help submarines breathe, listen, and communicate, and identifies cable-handling systems; the exact product names were not released.

Develops, integrates, produces, and supports AN/BYG-1 submarine combat-control capabilities, including weapon-launch hardware and software, trainers/simulators, and systems engineering. AN/BYG-1 is fielded on Virginia-class submarines.
NAICS-6 title: Research and Development in the Physical, Engineering, and Life Sciences (except Nanotechnology and Biotechnology)

Role / Description: Fabricates large, complex, and fracture-critical steel structures for Virginia- and Columbia-class submarines. Its work includes plate cutting and forming, precision machining, welding, and assembly.

Designs and manufactures critical electronic and electromechanical equipment for Virginia-class Blocks I-V. Public parts data links Aish terminal-board hardware to a Virginia-class data-acquisition unit, although the exact subaward configuration is not disclosed.

The exact Virginia-class component could not be confirmed publicly. Deltak is a custom metal fabricator providing laser/plasma cutting, forming, machining, robotic welding, and contract manufacturing.
NAICS-6 title: Ornamental and Architectural Metal Work Manufacturing

Role / Description: Supplies Level I Navy seamless and welded fittings, forgings, and pipe. Public transaction materials identify Custom Alloy as a dual source for Virginia-class main-seawater-system fittings.
NAICS-6 title: Fabricated Pipe and Pipe Fitting Manufacturing

Role / Description: Public parts-catalog data links Waukesha bearing hardware and associated temperature-sensing/probe components to Virginia-class submarine systems. The exact bearing assembly covered by this subaward was not publicly disclosed.

The exact Virginia-class deliverable could not be confirmed publicly. Rhoads performs heavy and custom metal fabrication, machining, welding, and shipboard/maritime construction and repair for Navy customers.
The subaward record establishes participation in the Virginia-class program, but no reliable public source was found that identifies this company's specific component, material, or service.
NAICS-6 title: Not confirmed from reliable public sources

Role / Description: The exact Virginia-class item could not be confirmed publicly. Foster-Miller/QinetiQ provides undersea systems engineering, robotics, advanced materials, and other defense technologies, but reviewed sources did not tie a specific deliverable to this subaward.
NAICS-6 title: Other Aircraft Parts and Auxiliary Equipment Manufacturing

Role / Description: Supplied advanced low-maintenance valve-regulated lead-acid main storage battery systems for Virginia-class submarines. Public descriptions state that these batteries provide emergency/backup power and reactor-restart capability, including cells and associated packaging/trays.
Manufactures critical fittings and assembled components for Virginia- and Columbia-class submarines. It also performs final machining and assembly of additively manufactured submarine parts.
NAICS-6 title: Other Metal Valve and Pipe Fitting Manufacturing

Role / Description: Manufactured large, complex steel castings for the Virginia-class supply chain. Public company sources confirm Americast's large engineered steel-casting business, but no reliable public source identified the exact casting covered by this subaward.

Provides large-scale precision fabrication, welding, and machining for BAE's Virginia-class propulsor assemblies. A disclosed subcontract identifies Ranor as supplying material to the propulsor contract, but does not name the exact part.

Supplies precision, cleanable all-metal wire-mesh filters and strainers for naval-nuclear submarine fluid systems. Public reporting connects the company's precision filters to Virginia-class construction, but does not identify the exact filter covered by this subaward.

Supplies Virginia-class shaft/bulkhead seal equipment and technical oversight for installation and maintenance. Wärtsilä's U.S. defense business specializes in marine shaft sealing and submarine propeller repair.

Fabricates massive structural-steel tooling and assemblies used by Electric Boat workers to build Virginia-class submarines. Public reporting does not identify the individual structures covered by this subaward.
NAICS-6 title: All Other Miscellaneous Fabricated Metal Product Manufacturing

Role / Description: Produces cast metal components for submarine propulsion, fluid-control, weapons, and structural-support systems. Public examples include shaft sleeves, valve components, propeller hubs, and HY-80 steel castings; the exact Virginia-class casting is not public.

Supplies Virginia-class salinity sensors, including Parker part number 106107-00 identified in public procurement notices. The sensors support monitoring of water/salinity conditions aboard the submarine.
NAICS-6 title: Commercial and Service Industry Machinery Manufacturing

Role / Description: The specific Virginia-class chemical or material could not be confirmed publicly. Evonik manufactures specialty additives, hardeners, silanes, and monomers for marine and protective coatings, but no reviewed source tied a particular product to this subaward.
NAICS-6 title: All Other Miscellaneous Chemical Product and Preparation Manufacturing

Role / Description: Provides U.S. Navy interior doors and closures fabricated in steel, aluminum, and stainless steel, plus related marine sheet-metal and repair work. Public sources did not identify the exact Virginia-class door or service covered by this subaward.

Manufactures welded and machined Navy hardware such as specialty fittings, flanges, union nuts, tees, caps, tailpieces, valves, and other machine components. The exact Virginia-class item was not publicly disclosed.

Provides SUBSAFE-authorized submarine modernization and repair plus precision fabrication, including installation, troubleshooting, maintenance, and repair of hull, mechanical and electrical, weapons, and main/auxiliary equipment. Public contracts also cover engineering and technical support for Virginia-class construction and maintenance.

Supplies fiber-optic and electrical cable assemblies, harnesses, and interconnect engineering, fabrication, installation, and logistics for military platforms. The exact Virginia-class cable assembly covered by this subaward was not publicly identified.

The exact Virginia-class deliverable could not be confirmed publicly. Leidos provides undersea-warfare systems engineering, integration, and test support; public parts data has linked Leidos cable assemblies to Virginia-class weapon-system applications, but the entity/configuration match is not definitive.

Produces precision-machined SUBSAFE/Level I, nuclear-plant, and non-level fittings, valves, flanges, components, and assemblies for U.S. submarines. The exact Virginia-class fitting or part number in this subaward was not publicly disclosed.

Public NSN data identifies a JAL electric shipboard range as an end item for Virginia Block III submarines. JAL also supplies shipboard electrical/power equipment; the exact subaward configuration was not publicly disclosed.

The exact Virginia-class item could not be confirmed publicly. Hutchinson manufactures naval-grade elastomeric shock/vibration isolation mounts and acoustic-damping materials used to protect equipment and reduce structure-borne noise.
NAICS-6 title: Rubber Product Manufacturing for Mechanical Use

Role / Description: Fabricates, machines, and integrates large, complex metal parts and structures for submarine programs. Public sources do not identify the exact Virginia-class component covered by this subaward.
NAICS-6 title: All Other Miscellaneous Fabricated Metal Product Manufacturing

Role / Description: Supplies forged metal components for defense and submarine applications. The exact Virginia-class forging covered by this subaward was not publicly identified.
Supplies shipboard electrical-distribution hardware, specifically marine load centers and switchboards installed on Virginia-class submarines, together with associated design and engineering support.
NAICS-6 title: Power, Distribution, and Specialty Transformer Manufacturing

Role / Description: Supplies marine valves installed on Virginia-class submarines. Public sources confirm the program relationship but do not identify the exact valve types covered by this subaward.

Manufactured pipe fittings, including elbow joints, for Virginia-class steam-piping systems. Public records identify NuFlo fittings as installed in the class, although they do not map this subaward to individual part numbers.

Provides heavy-lift marine transport, barge service, crane and rigging, specialized hauling, and nuclear-certified machinery/equipment transport for Virginia-class construction. Public records tie its work to SSN 802 and SSN 803.
NAICS-6 title: Coastal and Great Lakes Freight Transportation

Role / Description: Supplies shock-qualified rugged electronics enclosures and consoles used to house mission-critical naval electronics. The exact Virginia-class enclosure configuration was not publicly identified.
NAICS-6 title: All Other Miscellaneous Fabricated Metal Product Manufacturing

Role / Description: The exact Virginia-class item could not be confirmed publicly. The company is a fabricated-structural-metal manufacturer and appears as a subcontractor on Virginia-class construction, but available sources do not identify the specific fabrication.

Builds mission-critical welded, fabricated, and machined components supporting Virginia-class Blocks III-V construction and modernization. The exact component covered by this subaward was not publicly identified.
NAICS-6 title: Other Guided Missile and Space Vehicle Parts and Auxiliary Equipment Manufacturing

Role / Description: Produces precision-machined Level I/SUBSAFE critical components for Virginia- and Columbia-class submarines, including sole-source parts. The exact component covered by this subaward was not publicly disclosed.

The exact Virginia-class deliverable could not be confirmed publicly. Paradigm manufactures complex precision-machined and fabricated components for aerospace, power-generation, and marine markets, but reviewed sources did not tie a specific product to this subaward.

Supplies more than 150 components on USS New Jersey (SSN 796), including complex valve manifolds and other motion- and flow-control components. These products manage fluid and actuation functions across submarine systems.
NAICS-6 title: Fluid Power Valve and Hose Fitting Manufacturing

Role / Description: Supplies industrial-grade Siemens/Ruggedcom networking hardware and components for Virginia-class hull, mechanical, and electrical networks. The exact network configuration covered by this subaward was not publicly disclosed.
Supplies Level I specialty-alloy forgings, fittings, and pipe through the acquired Custom Alloy business, including Virginia-class main-seawater-system fittings.
NAICS-6 title: Fabricated Pipe and Pipe Fitting Manufacturing

Role / Description: The exact Virginia-class item could not be confirmed publicly. TiFab is a long-time U.S. Navy shipbuilding supplier that fabricates titanium, zirconium, and nickel-alloy pressure vessels, piping spools, heat exchangers, pump housings, and valve bodies.

Performs heavy fabrication and welding of large-scale, complex components for Virginia-class submarines. Public sources confirm class support but do not identify the specific installed component associated with this row.

Supplies resilient weapon-system mounts installed on Virginia-class submarines. Federal logistics cross-reference data links Oil States part numbers 5MC10000-H and ND8714 to Electric Boat’s SSN-774 mount assembly.
NAICS-6 title: Rubber Product Manufacturing for Mechanical Use

Role / Description: Supplies linear electrical accelerometers for Virginia-class submarines. Federal logistics data identifies PCB part 631M15/003JV (NSN 6680-01-589-2611) as an SSN-774 end-item component.

Performs precision machining, welding, fabrication, and assembly for Electric Boat submarine work; public vendor records identify products such as pump housings, manifolds, and submarine torque tooling. No public source ties a specific part number to this Virginia-class row.

Supplies heavy fabricated material for the Virginia-class propulsor forward assembly. A disclosed AT&F subcontract sits under BAE’s Navy contract for Virginia-class propulsor forward-subassembly material, manufacturing, and delivery.

Machines long, tight-tolerance submarine parts for Electric Boat using a dedicated facility built to bore and hone long components. Public sources confirm submarine manufacturing support but do not identify the specific Virginia-class part.

Supplies electrical-resistance temperature “lower probe” assemblies used in Virginia-class submarine engineering and diesel systems. Federal logistics data identifies Waukesha part C370-023 and related part F061-000-033 item 168 for SSN-774; a public source for the exact bearing assembly monitored was not found.

This UEI is Northrop Grumman Marine Systems in Sunnyvale, California. Northrop’s public filings identify the business as a producer of Virginia-class main-propulsion and ship-service turbine-generator equipment; the exact subaward line item is not publicly separated further.

This UEI maps to Northrop Grumman’s Sykesville, Maryland operation, which manufactures specialized electronic, electromechanical, and control assemblies. I found no public source identifying the exact Virginia-class construction component attributable to this entity, so a platform-specific part should not be inferred.

This UEI is the Alliant Techsystems/Northrop Grumman Rocket Center, West Virginia propulsion-products operation. Its public business is missile and rocket propulsion, and I found no credible public evidence of a Virginia-class submarine-construction role for this exact entity.
NAICS-6 title: Guided Missile and Space Vehicle Propulsion Unit and Propulsion Unit Parts Manufacturing

Role / Description: Northrop Grumman’s Annapolis undersea-systems entity supplies acoustic and sonar-related hardware. A Navy notice tied to this UEI identifies an acoustic sonar baffle, providing a concrete submarine construction item, although the public notice does not name the specific Virginia-class hull.

VACCO Industries supplies critical-quiet fluid-control valves, manifolds, and filtration assemblies for Virginia-class submarines. VACCO reported nearly 40 of its valves/manifolds aboard USS North Dakota (SSN-784), distinguishing this entity as a quiet-valve and manifold supplier.
NAICS-6 title: Fluid Power Valve and Hose Fitting Manufacturing

Role / Description: Westland Technologies manufactures molded elastomeric submarine products, including acoustic/sonar baffles, hull treatments, and vibration- or signature-reduction components. A Navy notice tied to this UEI identifies an acoustic sonar baffle, while Westland lists the Virginia class among its supported programs.
NAICS-6 title: Rubber Product Manufacturing for Mechanical Use

Role / Description: Johnson Controls Navy Systems is the York, Pennsylvania naval HVAC/refrigeration entity. It designs and manufactures submarine-qualified chillers, compressors, controls, and other environmental-control equipment; public sources do not identify the exact Virginia-class unit in this subaward.
NAICS-6 title: Air-Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment and Commercial and Industrial Refrigeration Equipment Manufacturing

Role / Description: This is the legacy York International registration at the same York, Pennsylvania HVAC/refrigeration complex. I could not distinguish a separate Virginia-class deliverable from Johnson Controls Navy Systems; the public record supports YORK-branded HVAC/R equipment but not a unique component for this UEI.
NAICS-6 title: Air-Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment and Commercial and Industrial Refrigeration Equipment Manufacturing

Role / Description: Curtiss-Wright’s Cheswick, Pennsylvania electro-mechanical operation manufactures nuclear-fleet pumps, motors, generators, and related propulsion-plant machinery. The exact Virginia-class machine represented by this subaward is not publicly itemized, so the role cannot be narrowed beyond that equipment family.

This UEI is Curtiss-Wright’s Wellsville, New York Steam & Air Solutions operation. It manufactures naval steam turbines, turbine-generator components, and high-pressure air compressors; public sources do not identify which Virginia-class unit was supplied under this subaward.
NAICS-6 title: Turbine and Turbine Generator Set Units Manufacturing

Role / Description: DY4/Curtiss-Wright Defense Solutions in Ashburn supplies rugged embedded computing hardware, including VPX single-board computers and processing modules. A Navy notice tied to the entity identifies a VPX computer, but the precise Virginia-class subsystem using it is not publicly disclosed.

Exlar manufactures electromechanical linear and rotary actuators. Navy procurement records tied to this UEI identify actuator hardware, but I found no public source identifying the exact Virginia-class valve, control, or machinery application.

This UEI is Moog’s Orrville, Ohio AMPAC-ISP operation, whose distinct product line is fluid-power cylinders and actuators. I found no public source naming the exact Virginia-class actuator supplied by this site, so the program role can be confirmed only at the fluid-power-actuation level.
NAICS-6 title: Fluid Power Cylinder and Actuator Manufacturing

Role / Description: This UEI is Moog’s East Aurora, New York headquarters/controls operation, which develops precision motion-control electronics, servo controls, and actuation systems. The public record does not identify a separate Virginia-class component for this UEI, so it should not be assigned the Orrville sibling’s hydraulic-cylinder role or the Blacksburg sibling’s motor/electronics role.
NAICS-6 title: Other Aircraft Parts and Auxiliary Equipment Manufacturing

Role / Description: This UEI is Moog’s Blacksburg, Virginia space-and-defense electronics operation, which manufactures motors, motor controls, circuit cards, and other electromechanical electronics. Navy notices confirm circuit-card and actuator-related items from the site, but not the exact Virginia-class subsystem represented by the subaward.

This UEI is CIRCOR Naval Solutions/Warren Pumps in Warren, Massachusetts. It supplies engineered centrifugal pumps and pump components for submarine cooling, seawater, auxiliary, and other fluid systems; the exact Virginia-class pump service in this subaward is not publicly identified.
NAICS-6 title: Measuring, Dispensing, and Other Pumping Equipment Manufacturing

Role / Description: This UEI resolves to the IMO Pump Division in Monroe, North Carolina, not Warren Pumps. It manufactures rotary/screw pump units and pump components for naval fluid systems; public notices confirm pump-unit supply, but do not identify the exact Virginia-class service.
NAICS-6 title: Measuring, Dispensing, and Other Pumping Equipment Manufacturing

Role / Description: Hale Hamilton in the United Kingdom manufactures high-pressure gas and air regulating, reducing, and shutoff valves. Navy procurement records confirm regulating-valve hardware from this entity, but the exact Virginia-class gas/air system application is not publicly identified.

This UEI is CIRCOR Aerospace’s Corona, California operation rather than a CIRCOR naval pump or valve plant. I found no public evidence confirming a distinct Virginia-class construction component for this entity.
NAICS-6 title: Aircraft Engine and Engine Parts Manufacturing

Role / Description: This UEI maps to CIRCOR Instrumentation Technologies’ Hoke/Gyrolok operation in Spartanburg, South Carolina. It supplies instrumentation valves, needle valves, manifolds, and tube fittings; public procurement evidence supports shipboard valve hardware, but does not identify the exact Virginia-class installation.

This UEI is an L3 Technologies corporate/Camden registration associated with communications and electronic systems contracting. I found no public evidence that separates a concrete Virginia-class deliverable for this entity from the affiliated Henschel, KEO, Chesapeake Sciences, or maritime-power operating units.
NAICS-6 title: Radio and Television Broadcasting and Wireless Communications Equipment Manufacturing

Role / Description: L3Harris Henschel’s Norfolk operation supplies submarine machinery-control and monitoring equipment, including consoles, control panels, alarms, and related ship-control electronics. The exact Virginia-class cabinet or console in this subaward is not publicly itemized.

Power Paragon in Gardena, California designs naval static power-conversion and electrical-distribution equipment. Public sources establish that capability, but I found no public evidence identifying the particular Virginia-class converter, inverter, transformer, or distribution unit represented by this subaward.
NAICS-6 title: Power, Distribution, and Specialty Transformer Manufacturing

Role / Description: L3Harris Maritime Services in Norfolk provides shipyard production and installation labor—welding, pipefitting, shipfitting, blasting/painting, and equipment installation. The exact Virginia-class new-construction work package is not publicly disclosed.

The Tulsa L3Harris maritime-power operation manufactures MIL-SPEC switchboards, load centers, and related shipboard power-distribution assemblies. Its Virginia-class role is electrical distribution equipment, although the precise assembly in the subaward is not publicly identified.

This UEI is L3 KEO in Northampton, Massachusetts, a photonics and submarine-mast business. It supplies optical/periscope and Universal Modular Mast hardware for Virginia-class submarines; public sources do not identify the exact mast module in this subaward.
NAICS-6 title: Search, Detection, Navigation, Guidance, Aeronautical, and Nautical System and Instrument Manufacturing

Role / Description: This UEI is L3 Chesapeake Sciences in Millersville, Maryland, which develops undersea sensors, acoustic systems, and submarine communications/mast electronics. Public Navy records confirm undersea electronics from the entity, but do not identify the exact Virginia-class assembly represented by the subaward.
NAICS-6 title: Search, Detection, Navigation, Guidance, Aeronautical, and Nautical System and Instrument Manufacturing

Role / Description: SPD Electrical Systems in Camden supplies shock-hardened naval circuit breakers, switchgear, and electrical-protection hardware. For Virginia-class construction, its distinct contribution is breaker/switchgear hardware rather than complete switchboards or machinery-control consoles.
